LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTORS AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IN PLAINTIFF WILLIAM F. WEBSTER JR.'S COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE IMPLIED WARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY CLAIM AND WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE IMPLIED WARRANTY OF FITNESS CLAIM. SEE MINUTES AND/OR TRANSCRIPT FOR DETAILS. MR. WEBSTER IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ADEQUATELY ALLEGE EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL WHICH MAY SHOW THAT HIS MERCHANTABILITY CLAIM IS NOT TIME-BARRED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: IRENE TOFFT, CSR # 12313

LAW AND MOTION, THE COURT MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULES AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF WILLIAM WEBSTER JR.'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES, COSTS AND EXPENSES IS GRANTED IN PART. MR. WEBSTER IS AWARDED FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $8,975.00 AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $977.15 FOR A TOTAL AMOUNT OF $9,952.15 FOR. THE COURT AWARDS FEES AND COSTS FOR THE FEES AND COSTS INCURRED AS OF THE DATE OF THE 998 OFFER OF 10/17/17. THE COURT ACCEPTS AS REASONABLE BOTH THE HOURS WORKED AND THE HOURLY RATES CLAIMED BY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. THE COURT FINDS, HOWEVER, THAT AFTER THE DATE OF THE 998 OFFER, THE FEES AND COSTS INCURRED TO FURTHER LITIGATE THE CASE WERE NOT REASONABLE AND WERE NOT TO THE BENEFIT OF THE THE PLAINTIFF HIMSELF BUT TO THE BENEFIT OF PLAINTIFF'S ATTORNEYS. THE COURT INDICATES THAT THERE IS NO MEMORANDUM OF COSTS PROECEEDING AS THAT PROCEEDING HAS BEEN INCORPORATED INTO THE PRESENT MOTION. COUNSEL FOR EACH SIDE INDICATE THEIR INTENT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER FOR THE COURT TO CONSIDER. THE COURT INSTRUCTS THE PARTIES TO SERVE A COPY OF THEIR PROPOSED ORDER ON THE OTHER SIDE. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/HK)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expneses
Off Calendar Nov-29-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. DEEPAK DEVABOSE, ESQ. of Knight Law Group (Tel: 310-552-2250) appearing for plaintiff William F. Webster, Jr.;
Atty. KAINOA K. ALIVIADO, ESQ. of Theta Law Firm, LLP (Tel: 424-297-3103) appearing via CourtCall for defendant Hyundai Motor America.

Matter is argued and submitted by counsel for parties. The Court modifies its tentative ruling and rules as follows: Plaintiff William Webster Jr.'s motion for attorney's fees, costs and expenses is granted in part. Mr. Webster is awarded fees in the amount of $8,975.00 and costs in the amount of $977.15 for a total amount of $9,952.15 for. The Court awards fees and costs for the fees and costs incurred as of the date of the 998 offer of 10/17/17. The Court accepts as reasonable both the hours worked and the hourly rates claimed by plaintiff's counsel. The Court finds, however, that after the date of the 998 offer, the fees and costs incurred to further litigate the case were not reasonable and were not to the benefit of the the plaintiff himself but to the benefit of plaintiff's attorneys. The Court indicates that there is no memorandum of costs proeceeding as that proceeding has been incorporated into the present motion. Counsel for each side indicate their intent to prepare a proposed order for the Court to consider. The Court instructs the parties to serve a copy of their proposed order on the other side.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Sean Kane, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expneses

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-20-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-16-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-01-2017 Set For Trial On Sep-24-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Diane L. Hernandez of the law offices of Knight Law Group LLP (310-552-2250) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Kainoa K. Aliviado of the law offices of Theta Law Firm LLP (424-297-3103) appears as by telephone as counsel for defendant/demurring party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the second cause of action for breach of implied warranty in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff William Webster, Jr. is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. The FAC shows that the implied warranty claim is time-barred. Per Mexia v. Rinker Boat Co., Inc. (2009) 174 Cal. App. 4th 1297, the statute of limitations on the Mr. Webster's implied warranty claim ran no later than five years after he bought the car. Mr. Webster has not alleged sufficient facts to support either equitable estoppel or equitable tolling and, because he has now been given two chances to do, it does not appear that he can reasonably do so. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, e-mail: geales@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Same
Off Calendar Sep-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Diane L. Hernandez of the law offices of Knight Law Group LLP (310-552-2250) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Kainoa K. Aliviado of the law offices of Theta Law Firm LLP (424-297-3103) appears as by telephone as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA's Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Same is GRANTED without leave to amend. The portions of the FAC alleging implied warranty of fitness, equitable estoppel and equitable tolling are stricken. The court previously ruled that Mr. Webster cannot state a claim for implied warranty of fitness. Mr. Webster has not alleged sufficient facts to support either equitable estoppel or equitable tolling and, because he has now been given two chances to do, it does not appear that he can reasonably do so. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, e-mail: geales@sftc.org. (L&M302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2017 Continued To Nov-15-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTORS AMERICA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT as to the second cause of action for breach of implied warranty and paragraph 31 of plaintiff William F. Webster Jr.'s complaint is OFF CALENDAR as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene Tofft, CSR # 12313 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTORS AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as to the second cause of action in plaintiff William F. Webster Jr.'s complaint is Sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the implied warranty of merchantability claim and without leave to amend as to the implied warranty of fitness claim. The second cause of action alleges two distinct implied warranty claims which are treated separately for purposes of this demurrer. Hyundai has shown that both claims are barred by the four-year statute of limitations set forth in California Uniform Commercial Code 2725(1). (Krieger v. Nick Alexander Imports, Inc. (1991) 234 Cal.App.3d 205, 212-215.) The statute of limitations runs from the date of accrual, which is the point at which the breach occurs regardless of the consumer's knowledge or discovery. (Mexia v.Rinker Boat Co., Inc. (2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1297, 1304 and 1309-1310.) There is a one-year period for implied warranties, so the earliest time of accrual is at delivery and the latest is one year after delivery. (Id. at 1306 and 1309.) Assuming the one-year period applies-and putting aside any equitable estoppel argument-Mr. Webster was required to file his implied warranty claims by February 11, 2016. Mr. Webster argues the doctrine of equitable estoppel applies, but has not alleged any facts to support that doctrine. Mr. Webster is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to adequately allege equitable estoppel which may show that his merchantability claim is not time-barred. Mr. Webster failed to adequately allege a breach of implied warranty of fitness claim, nor is there any reasonably possibility that he can do so. Mr. Webster has not adequately alleged any of the second through fifth elements in CACI 3211. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene Tofft, CSR # 12313 =302/hk
